expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: heatmap highlighting effect scenarios cover activation, persistence, and reset behavior
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the plan explicitly enumerates scenarios for all three required behaviors: Activation (e.g., “Activate highlight by selecting a heatmap cell”, “Activate highlight by selecting a heatmap row/column header (if supported)”), Persistence (e.g., “Highlight persistence: remains highlighted when interacting outside the heatmap”, “Highlight persistence: switching between visualizations does not corrupt highlight state”), and Reset (e.g., “Reset highlight: clear selection via explicit clear action”, “Reset highlight: selecting a different cell updates highlight correctly”, “Reset highlight on data/viewport change”). It also explicitly states: “This benchmark’s focus … explicitly test: Activation … Persistence … Reset/clearing highlight.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md contains an explicit “Primary phase alignment: Phase 4a” section and states Phase 4a requires “subcategory-only draft” with “Atomic action chains” and “Observable verification leaves.” The provided plan is organized as “Heatmap — Highlight effect (iOS)” with bullet scenarios and nested step chains ending in observable checks (e.g., “The tapped cell enters the highlighted state (visually distinct)”). It also avoids canonical top categories, consistent with: “Output is intentionally Phase 4a subcategory-only (no canonical categories like EndToEnd/Compatibility/Security).”
